meta.description:
Stop manually chasing vendor status updates. This workflow sends scheduled ping emails to your vendors with four one-click status buttons (On Track, At Risk, Delayed, Needs PM Input). When a vendor clicks their button, the workflow validates their token and logs their response to a Google Sheet.

What this workflow does:
- Reads active vendors from a Google Sheet on a schedule
- Generates a unique token per vendor per cycle
- Sends a styled ping email with four status buttons
- Captures vendor clicks via webhook and validates the token
- Updates the vendor row in your Google Sheet with the response and timestamp
- Shows the vendor a confirmation page

The workflow preserves clarity — if a vendor does not respond, their row stays blank. Nothing is assumed.

Requirements:
- Google Sheets (one sheet with a Vendors tab — see node notes for required columns)
- Google Sheets OAuth2 credential in n8n
- SMTP email credential (Gmail with App Password works)
- n8n instance at a publicly accessible URL — vendors click links that point back to your n8n webhook
- Set webhookBaseUrl in the Config node to your public n8n webhook base URL

Privacy note: Vendor data is stored in your Google Sheet and sent through your configured email provider. The workflow runs in your own n8n instance. Do not use this for confidential vendor or project information unless your organization permits this workflow design and your n8n, Google, and email credentials are configured appropriately.

Community Edition includes the core one-click status capture pattern. The full Vendor Coordination Tracker adds comment capture, flagged-status PM alerts, weekly summary reporting, response threshold logic, setup guide, and sample Sheet templates.

Full version at pmexecution.com

name: Automate Vendor Status Updates with Tokenized Email Links

parameters.content:
## Automate Vendor Status Updates
## with Tokenized Email Links

### How it works

This workflow runs on a schedule to send status ping emails to your active vendors. Each email contains four one-click status buttons. When a vendor clicks a button, a webhook captures the response, validates the token, updates your Google Sheet, and shows the vendor a confirmation page.

### Setup steps

1. Open the **Config** node and set your n8n public webhook base URL and email address
2. Add your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential and replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID in all Sheets nodes
3. Add your SMTP credential and set your From Email in the Send Ping Email node
4. Create your Google Sheet with the required columns (see Sheets node notes)
5. Activate the workflow — both the schedule and the webhook become live
6. Test with one vendor row before sending to real vendors

### Required Google Sheet columns

```
vendor_id
vendor_name
contact_name
contact_email
project_name
scope
status
ping_token
ping_sent_at
response_status
last_response_at
```

Column names are case-sensitive.

### Community Edition

This version captures one-click status only. The full product at pmexecution.com adds a vendor comment form, PM alert emails for flagged statuses, weekly PM summary reports, and a complete setup guide.

## Branch 1 — Scheduled Ping

Runs every Monday at 8am. Reads active vendors from Google Sheets, generates a unique one-time token per vendor, writes the token to the sheet, and sends a ping email with four status buttons. Edit the **Config** node before activating.

## Branch 2 — Vendor Response Capture

Always-on webhook. Fires when a vendor clicks a status button in their ping email. Validates the one-time token against the sheet, updates the vendor row with status and timestamp, and serves a confirmation page. Invalid or expired tokens get an error page.

nodes.name: Every Monday at 8am
nodes.notes: Runs every Monday at 8am. Change the cron expression to adjust the schedule. Format: minute hour day month weekday. Example for daily at 9am: 0 9 * * *

nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: START HERE. Replace webhookBaseUrl with your public n8n webhook URL and pmEmail with your email address. Save before activating.

nodes.name: Read Vendors from Sheet
nodes.notes: Reads all rows from the Vendors tab. Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID with your Sheet ID from the URL. Required columns: vendor_id, vendor_name, contact_name, contact_email, project_name, scope, status, ping_token, ping_sent_at, response_status, last_response_at. Select your Google Sheets credential.

nodes.name: Filter Active Vendors Only
nodes.notes: Only processes rows where status = Active. Set a vendor row to Inactive, On Hold, or Closed to stop pings.

nodes.name: Generate Tokens and Status Links
nodes.notes: Creates a unique 32-character token per vendor. Builds four status links with the token embedded. Enforces required vendor_id and contact_email fields. Reads webhook base URL from Config node.

nodes.name: Write Token to Sheet
nodes.notes: Writes the token and ping timestamp to the vendor row BEFORE sending the email. Clears prior response fields. If this write fails, the email will not send — preventing vendors from receiving links that can never validate.

nodes.name: Preserve Vendor Payload
nodes.notes: Google Sheets update nodes only return the columns they wrote, dropping the status links and contact fields. This node restores the full generated payload so the email node has access to all fields.

nodes.name: Send Ping Email to Vendor
nodes.notes: Sends a styled HTML ping email with four status buttons. Select your Gmail SMTP credential. Update From Email to your sending address.

parameters.subject: ={{ $json.project_name + ' — Status Update Request: ' + $json.vendor_name }}

nodes.name: When Vendor Clicks Status Link
nodes.notes: Always-on GET webhook. Path: vendor-status. Use the Production URL — not the Test URL.

nodes.name: Read Sheet to Validate Token
nodes.notes: Reads the full Vendors tab to find the row matching the vendor_id from the URL and validate the token. Same Sheet ID and credential as Branch 1.

nodes.name: Validate Token and Status
nodes.notes: Matches vendor_id and token from URL params against the stored token. Whitelists the four valid status values. Returns valid: true/false with vendor details.

nodes.name: Write Status to Sheet
nodes.notes: Updates the vendor row with their response status and timestamp. Clears the token so it cannot be reused.

nodes.name: Build Confirmation Page
nodes.notes: Builds a clean confirmation page showing the vendor's status badge, project name, and vendor name. All values are HTML-escaped.

parameters.jsCode:
const html = `

Link unavailable

This status link has expired or is no longer valid.

Please reply directly to the status request email or contact your project manager.

Powered by PM Execution Tools

`; return [{ json: { errorHtml: html } }];
